```json
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The paper asserts a clear contribution claim ('advances the state of the art') and a gap statement; whether those claims are coherently grounded in the artifact text must be checked.",
    "R1.2": "The experimental logic has internal tension: the token-length confound is partially acknowledged but not ruled out; the step from three-random-ordering stability to 'marker causes improvement' is an inferential leap worth checking.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: all three baselines are variants of the authors' own FreeForm-CoT family, no published external CoT baseline (e.g., Wei et al. 2022) appears; the sweeping 'clearly outperforms prior approaches' claim is not supported by the evidence as presented — the alignment between claim and evidence is the most critical failure risk.",
    "R1.4": "Load-bearing: 'SOTA' novelty is claimed, but the comparison set is exclusively the authors' own prior work under three anonymized variants; assessing whether the delta is meaningful against the actual prior-work frontier is essential.",
    "R1.7": "All baselines cite 'Anonymous, 2023a' — the authors' own prior submission — with no coverage of the published chain-of-thought literature; scholarship gaps directly bear on the novelty and framing claims."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this check focus on scientific/methodological validity (evaluating claims, evidence alignment, and novelty), or on writing and language quality (prose clarity, grammar, and style)?"
}
```

rationale.R1.1: The paper asserts a clear contribution claim ('advances the state of the art') and a gap statement; whether those claims are coherently grounded in the artifact text must be checked.
rationale.R1.2: The experimental logic has internal tension: the token-length confound is partially acknowledged but not ruled out; the step from three-random-ordering stability to 'marker causes improvement' is an inferential leap worth checking.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: all three baselines are variants of the authors' own FreeForm-CoT family, no published external CoT baseline (e.g., Wei et al. 2022) appears; the sweeping 'clearly outperforms prior approaches' claim is not supported by the evidence as presented — the alignment between claim and evidence is the most critical failure risk.
rationale.R1.4: Load-bearing: 'SOTA' novelty is claimed, but the comparison set is exclusively the authors' own prior work under three anonymized variants; assessing whether the delta is meaningful against the actual prior-work frontier is essential.
rationale.R1.7: All baselines cite 'Anonymous, 2023a' — the authors' own prior submission — with no coverage of the published chain-of-thought literature; scholarship gaps directly bear on the novelty and framing claims.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this check focus on scientific/methodological validity (evaluating claims, evidence alignment, and novelty), or on writing and language quality (prose clarity, grammar, and style)?
